sourceMovie.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Grand Budapest Hotel for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wes Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Grand Budapest Hotel clicked for you, The Lobster (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Grand Budapest Hotel?
faq.answer: Try The Lobster, Bottle Rocket, Hair—each shares DNA with The Grand Budapest Hotel while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Grand Budapest Hotel?
faq.answer: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Grand Budapest Hotel worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
